LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS FLIGHTEN UP, LLC, CHRIS MICHAILOV, AND RYAN BORLAND'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINANT GOWER SMITHS ENTIRE COMPLAINT AND EACH AND EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION THEREOF UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16 HELD ON APR-13-2015, IN DEPT. 302. PLAINTIFFS FILED AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AGAINST G. SMITH'S CROSS-COMPLAINT. G. SMITH SUBSEQUENTLY DISMISSED THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, SO THIS COURT LOSES JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THE MOTION. THE COURT AWARDS PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS UNDER CCP SEC. 425.16 IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,500.00. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT ZOOMSYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA H. HAFFNER APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. MATTHEW K. YAN OF MOSCONE EMBLIDGE & OTIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ZOOMSYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to the 1st, 3rd, 4th, 5th Causes of Action. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 7th Cause of action. 22 of the complaint states "defendant ZoomSystems disclosed plaintiff's business idea' This conduct breached the non-disclosure agreement." Plaintiff states they have sustained damage due to the breach. Thus, P has properly alleged breach of contract. Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 1st Cause of Action. The elements that must be pleaded under a fraud cause of action are: "(a) misrepresentation (false representation, concealment or nondisclosure); "(b) knowledge of falsity (or 'scienter'); "(c) intent to defraud, i.e., to induce reliance; "(d) justifiable reliance; and "(e) resulting damage." [See Philipson & Simon v. Gulsvig (2007) 154 CA4th 347, 363.] Here, in the complaint D has alleged (1) misrepresentation; (2) knowledge of falsity; (3) intent to defraud; (4) reliance; and (5) resulting damage. (See Complaint 24, 48, 51.) Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 3rd Cause of Action. "Before a person can be charged with a fiduciary obligation, he must either knowingly undertake to act on behalf and for the benefit of another, or must enter into a relationship which imposes that undertaking as a matter of law." (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 221, 197 Cal.Rptr. 783, 673 P.2d 660 (Children's Television ).) City of Hope Nat. Med. Ctr. v. Genentech, Inc., (2008) 43 Cal. 4th 375, 386. Here, Plaintiff has alleged it entrusted D with a confidential business plan and parties has executed a nondisclosure agreement to that effect, creating a fiduciary agreement between the parties. Thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 4th Cause of Action. The Unfair Competition Law (UCL) is not limited to anticompetitive business practices. "Unfair competition" under the UCL includes "any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent" business practice. [Bus. & Prof.C. 17200] Here, P has properly pled business practices forbidden by law, thus, the demurrer is overruled as to the 5th Cause of Action. Generally, conversion requires interference with tangible property, there can be no conversion of the goodwill of a business, or trade secrets. (Olschewski v. Hudson (1927) 87 C.A. 282, 286, 262 P. 43; see Rest.2d, Torts 242, Comment f; on equitable relief for improper use of trade secrets, see 13 Summary (10th), Equity, 83, 89.; See 5 Witkin, Summary 10th (2005) Torts, 702, p. 1025. Here, Plaintiff has not alleged Defendant interfered with tangible property, thus the demurrer is sustained as to the 7th Cause of Action. Defendant was given 20 days leave to file an answer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complainant Gower Smiths Entire Complaint And Each And Every Cause Of Action Thereof Under Ccp Section 425.16
Off Calendar Apr-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSHUA H. HAFFNER of Kabateck Brown Kellner, LLP, Tel No. (213) 217-5000 appearing telephonically for Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants Flighten Up, LLC, Chris Michailov, and Ryan Borland;
Atty. RYAN W. STAHL of Scherer Smith & Kenny LLP, Tel No. (415) 433-1099 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Gower Smith, and Defendants Lincoln Smith, Jordan Swyft, and Swyft, Inc.

RULING - Argument presented by counsel as to the issue of award of attorney's fees and costs. Matter submitted. The Court ruled as follows: As to CROSS-DEFENDANTS FLIGHTEN UP, LLC, CHRIS MICHAILOV, AND RYAN BORLAND'S Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complainant Gower Smiths Entire Complaint And Each And Every Cause Of Action Thereof Under CCP Section 425.16, Plaintiffs filed an anti-SLAPP motion against G. Smith's cross-complaint. G. Smith subsequently dismissed the cross-complaint, so this Court loses jurisdiction to rule on the motion. The Court awards Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants attorneys fees and costs under CCP sec. 425.16 in the amount of $10,500.00. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)
